title: sphaeralcea ambigua subsp. rosacea
intro: Sphaeralcea ambigua subsp. rosacea, also known as Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It belongs to the family Malvaceae and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Desert Marigold can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in folk remedies, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
